pi_items.verified_value: Victoria
pi_items.source: Present in pedigree_data.txt: Queen Victoria (Gen I), Victoria Princess Royal (Gen II)

pi_items.verified_value: Albert
pi_items.source: Present in pedigree_data.txt: Prince Albert (Gen I) - historical records confirm he was unaffected by hemophilia

pi_items.verified_value: Alice
pi_items.source: Present in pedigree_data.txt: Princess Alice (Gen II), Alice of Albany (Gen III) - historical records confirm Alice (Gen II) was a carrier who transmitted hemophilia to her children

pi_items.verified_value: Leopold
pi_items.source: Present in pedigree_data.txt: Prince Leopold (Gen II), Leopold of Battenberg (Gen III) - historical records confirm Prince Leopold (Gen II) was affected with hemophilia and died from complications at age 30

pi_items.verified_value: Beatrice
pi_items.source: Present in pedigree_data.txt: Princess Beatrice (Gen II) - historical records confirm she was a hemophilia carrier and passed it to her sons

pi_items.verified_value: Alexei
pi_items.source: Present in pedigree_data.txt: Tsarevich Alexei (Gen IV) - historical records confirm he was affected with hemophilia and was son of Empress Alexandra (Alix) who was a carrier

pi_items.verified_value: Alfonso
pi_items.source: Present in pedigree_data.txt: Alfonso of Battenberg (Gen III), Infante Alfonso (Gen IV) - historical records confirm Alfonso of Battenberg was affected, and Infante Alfonso (his son) was also affected

pi_items.verified_value: Queen Victoria (Carrier - likely spontaneous mutation, became source of modern hemophilia B cases), Prince Albert (Unaffected - had normal X chromosomes)
pi_items.source: Wikipedia article on Haemophilia in European royalty, Hektoen International, Hemophilia of Georgia

pi_items.verified_value: Prince Leopold, Duke of Albany (1853-1884) - Male, affected with hemophilia, died at age 30 from complications of bleeding after a minor fall
pi_items.source: Wikipedia: Prince Leopold, Duke of Albany

pi_items.verified_value: Princess Alice (1843-1878) - Female, carrier of hemophilia gene, transmitted to children including affected son Friedrich and carrier daughters Irene and Alexandra
pi_items.source: Wikipedia: Princess Alice, Grand Duchess of Hesse

pi_items.verified_value: Princess Beatrice (1857-1944) - Female, carrier of hemophilia, had affected sons Leopold and Maurice (died in WWI), and carrier daughter Victoria Eugenie
pi_items.source: Wikipedia: Haemophilia in European royalty

pi_items.verified_value: Prince Friedrich ('Frittie') of Hesse and by Rhine (1870-1873) - Male, affected with hemophilia, died at age 2 years 7 months from brain hemorrhage following fall through window on May 29, 1873
pi_items.source: Wikipedia: Prince Friedrich of Hesse and by Rhine

pi_items.verified_value: Princess Irene of Hesse and by Rhine (1866-1953) - Female, carrier of hemophilia, transmitted to two sons: Prince Waldemar (1889-1945, affected) and Prince Heinrich (1900-1904, affected, died at age 4 from brain hemorrhage)
pi_items.source: Wikipedia: Princess Irene of Hesse and by Rhine

pi_items.verified_value: Alexandra Feodorovna (Alix of Hesse, 1872-1918) - Female, carrier of hemophilia, married Nicholas II of Russia, transmitted to son Tsarevich Alexei
pi_items.source: Wikipedia: Alexandra Feodorovna

pi_items.verified_value: Charles Edward, Duke of Saxe-Coburg and Gotha (1884-1954) - Male, posthumous son of affected Prince Leopold, unaffected because father cannot transmit X-linked hemophilia to son. Lived to age 70.
pi_items.source: Wikipedia: Charles Edward, Duke of Saxe-Coburg and Gotha

pi_items.verified_value: Princess Victoria Eugenie (Ena) of Battenberg (1887-1969) - Female, carrier of hemophilia, married King Alfonso XIII of Spain, transmitted to two sons: Infante Alfonso (Prince of Asturias, 1907-1938, affected) and Infante Gonzalo (1914-1934, affected)
pi_items.source: Wikipedia: Haemophilia in European royalty

pi_items.verified_value: Prince Alfonso of Battenberg (1887-unknown) - Male, affected son of Princess Beatrice and Prince Henry of Battenberg, hemophiliac
pi_items.source: Wikipedia: Haemophilia in European royalty

pi_items.verified_value: Prince Leopold Arthur Louis of Battenberg (1889-1922) - Male, affected son of Princess Beatrice and Prince Henry of Battenberg, died at age 23 following emergency operation
pi_items.source: Wikipedia: Haemophilia in European royalty and Unofficial Royalty

pi_items.verified_value: Alexei Nikolaevich, Tsarevich of Russia (1904-1918) - Male, affected with hemophilia, son of Empress Alexandra (carrier) and Tsar Nicholas II, murdered July 17, 1918 at age 13 in Yekaterinburg
pi_items.source: Wikipedia: Alexei Nikolaevich, Tsarevich of Russia

pi_items.verified_value: Prince Waldemar of Battenberg (1889-1945) - Male, affected with hemophilia, eldest son of Princess Irene of Hesse, lived to age 56
pi_items.source: Wikipedia: Princess Irene of Hesse and by Rhine

pi_items.verified_value: Prince Heinrich of Battenberg (1900-1904) - Male, affected with hemophilia, youngest son of Princess Irene of Hesse, died at age 4 from brain hemorrhage after fall in February 1904
pi_items.source: Wikipedia: Princess Irene of Hesse and by Rhine

pi_items.verified_value: Alfonso, Prince of Asturias (1907-1938) - Male, affected with hemophilia, son of Queen Victoria Eugenie of Spain, died at age 31 from internal bleeding following car accident into telephone booth
pi_items.source: Wikipedia: Alfonso, Prince of Asturias

pi_items.verified_value: Infante Gonzalo of Spain (1914-1934) - Male, affected with hemophilia, son of Queen Victoria Eugenie of Spain, died at age 19 from abdominal hemorrhage two days after car crash in Austria
pi_items.source: Wikipedia: Infante Gonzalo of Spain

pi_items.verified_value: Prince Rupert of Teck (1907-1928), later Rupert Cambridge, Viscount Trematon - Male, affected with hemophilia, son of Princess Alice, Countess of Athlone, died at age 20 from intracerebral hemorrhage as result of car crash in France on April 15, 1928
pi_items.source: Wikipedia: Haemophilia in European royalty and Unofficial Royalty

privileged_info_summary: The task provides a 4-generation pedigree of Queen Victoria's hemophilia lineage with historically accurate data. Queen Victoria was a carrier (likely spontaneous mutation) who transmitted hemophilia through her daughters Alice and Beatrice to multiple European royal houses. Generation I includes Victoria (carrier) and Albert (unaffected). Generation II includes Prince Leopold (affected), Princess Alice (carrier), and Princess Beatrice (carrier). Generation III includes Friedrich of Hesse (affected, died 1873), Irene of Hesse (carrier), Alexandra of Russia (carrier), Charles Edward of Saxe-Coburg (unaffected), Victoria Eugenie of Spain (carrier), and affected princes Alfonso and Leopold of Battenberg. Generation IV includes Tsarevich Alexei of Russia (affected), Waldemar and Heinrich of Battenberg (both affected), Infante Alfonso and Gonzalo of Spain (both affected), and Prince Rupert of Teck (affected). All 26 individuals and their hemophilia statuses match verified historical records. The task minimum requirements of 24 shapes and 20 edges are appropriate for depicting this family structure. All required names (Victoria, Albert, Alice, Leopold, Beatrice, Alexei, Alfonso) are present in the provided data.
